symptoms.symptom_name: New bilateral peripheral pitting oedema extending to the mid-thighs

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical dependent oedema reflects systemic venous congestion from right ventricular failure.
Bilateral symmetric oedema with systemic congestion is not a unilateral venous thrombosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal distension and a tight bloated belly with dull right-upper-quadrant ache

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Congestive hepatomegaly and ascites cause RUQ ache and distension in right heart failure.
Ascites/hepatomegaly overlap with liver disease; distinguishing needs examination (JVP) and investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive worsening exertional dyspnoea over 3 weeks in severe COPD

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Worsening breathlessness accompanies RV decompensation and worsening hypoxaemia.
Non-specific — also fits a COPD flare; needs the pertinent negatives to separate.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever and no new purulent sputum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/purulent sputum argues against a primary infective exacerbation driving the picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting hypoxaemia ~89% on a home fingertip oximeter with visible cyanosis and accessory-muscle use

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxaemia drives hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction and RV decompensation; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth but unverified and confounded by a chronic low baseline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated JVP (6 cm above sternal angle) with prominent v wave and palpable RV heave

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated JVP with a v wave and an RV heave localise the failure to the right ventricle — sealed clinician examination, unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loud pulmonary component of S2 (P2) and right-sided S3 on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A loud P2 reflects pulmonary hypertension; sealed auscultatory finding not obtainable in chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ascites (shifting dullness) and tender hepatomegaly on abdominal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Congestive hepatomegaly and ascites confirm systemic venous congestion; sealed examination finding.

investigation_details.expected_result: Right ventricular hypertrophy and dilatation with elevated estimated pulmonary artery pressure
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The structural confirmation of cor pulmonale is missed; diagnosis remains presumptive.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echo confirms RV involvement and estimates pulmonary pressure — hospital/outpatient investigation, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: decompensated cor pulmonale / right heart failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Elevated JVP + RV heave + loud P2 + echo RV hypertrophy is the diagnostic tetrad; every element requires in-person examination or investigation and is sealed from the telehealth-readable side.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Look for right heart strain/right axis and to help exclude an ischaemic or arrhythmic precipitant.
Confirms RV hypertrophy/strain and estimates pulmonary artery pressure — structural confirmation of cor pulmonale.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Electrolytes and renal function (basic metabolic panel)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline before and during diuresis; right heart failure and diuretics both threaten renal function and electrolytes.
Assess for hypercapnia/CO2 retention before oxygen titration in a chronic-lung-disease patient.
